sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: NN Inc stock surges on AI data center awards as strategic pivot gains traction
feed.summary: NN Inc (NASDAQ: NNBR) saw its stock surge by 19.3% after securing multiple contracts for liquid cooling components in AI data centers, marking a significant win in its strategic pivot from legacy automotive parts to high-growth tech sectors. This move validates the company's restructuring efforts and is attracting interest from DACH investors seeking stable US industrial exposure. Insider stock grants further underscore confidence in this AI-driven growth strategy as NN Inc targets nearly 100 new program launches in fiscal 2026.

feed.title: Live Nation CEO Michael Rapino, defending his company, takes star role at antitrust trial
feed.summary: Live Nation CEO Michael Rapino testified in a New York antitrust trial, defending his company's dominant position against claims of being greedy and abusive to customers. While the federal government and six states have settled, 33 states and D.C. are continuing their legal challenge. Rapino dismissed accusations of shutting out competitors and driving up prices, describing the industry as competitive with narrow profit margins, and addressed controversial internal messages and past ticketing issues.

feed.title: NextNRG Receives Nasdaq Notice on Minimum Bid Price
feed.summary: NextNRG (NXXT) has received a notice from Nasdaq for failing to meet the minimum $1.00 bid price requirement for 30 consecutive business days as of March 16, 2026. The company has until September 14, 2026, to regain compliance, potentially with measures like a reverse stock split. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, assesses NXXT as Neutral due to weak financial quality but acknowledges positive operational momentum.

feed.title: Bank of Nova Scotia (BNS) Expands Stake in KeyCorp (KEY)
feed.summary: Bank of Nova Scotia (BNS) plans to increase its stake in KeyCorp (KEY) to 19.99%, up from a previously approved 14.99%, without changing the existing relationship between the companies. BNS, a major financial services provider with an $84.08 billion market cap, demonstrates strong profitability and cash flow but faces concerns due to high debt levels and a low dividend yield. While valuation metrics suggest potential undervaluation and technical indicators point to an oversold status, analysts maintain a mixed "hold" sentiment.

feed.title: Trump Suit Against Capital One Dismissed But Can Be Refiled
feed.summary: A US District Judge dismissed the Trump Organization's lawsuit against Capital One, which accused the bank of political discrimination and improperly closing accounts. However, the judge allowed the Trump Organization to refile the suit if it amends its "deficient" complaint, and granted a 90-day period for discovery. The ruling is a mixed result, enabling the Trump Organization to gather evidence for a stronger case.

feed.title: Therapeutics Sector Q4 Results: Revenue Beats Amid Market Stability
feed.summary: A recent analysis of eleven therapeutics companies reveals that they collectively surpassed Q4 revenue estimates by 7.1%, despite stock prices remaining largely stable. Moderna, while experiencing a year-over-year decline, still beat analyst projections, and Novavax showed the strongest performance with a significant revenue increase due to its nanoparticle technology. The sector anticipates future growth driven by precision medicine and rare disease treatments, though it faces challenges from drug cost scrutiny and regulatory uncertainties.

feed.title: Apollo To Invest $1B In 500 Realty Income Single-Tenant Properties
feed.summary: Apollo Global Management will invest $1 billion for a 49% interest in a new joint venture with Realty Income Corp. This partnership will focus on a portfolio of approximately 500 single-tenant retail properties with long-term net leases, with Realty Income continuing to manage the assets. The deal, expected to close on March 31, is part of Realty Income's private capital initiative aimed at diversifying its capital sources.

feed.title: Leidos Secures Key Air Force Contract Amidst Analyst Revisions
feed.summary: Leidos Holdings Inc. has secured a significant $454.9 million U.S. Air Force contract for cloud modernization, leading to a 3.87% stock increase despite recent downward revisions in analyst price targets. The company maintains strong financial metrics, strategic partnerships, and a positive long-term outlook, although some sectors show slowing revenue. Traders are advised to monitor key support and resistance levels for potential entry or exit points.

feed.title: EyePoint sues rival Ocular Therapeutix, alleging false claims about eye drug
feed.summary: EyePoint Pharmaceuticals has filed a lawsuit against its competitor, Ocular Therapeutix, alleging that Ocular Therapeutix made false claims regarding its eye drug. The lawsuit highlights a legal dispute within the ophthalmology pharmaceutical sector between the two companies.

feed.title: Jayesh Sahasi sells ONTF common shares in three trades (ONTF)
feed.summary: Jayesh Sahasi, an insider at ON24 Inc. (ONTF), reported three instances of common stock sales. These transactions occurred between December 2025 and March 2026, totaling gross proceeds of $452,598.48. The sales appear to be routine and involved various share counts and proceeds on each date.

feed.title: Edwards Lifesciences Policy Hire Puts Valuation And Returns In Focus
feed.summary: Edwards Lifesciences has appointed Niranjan Pai as Director, Government Affairs, a move that places an experienced policy professional in a key role for public policy and regulatory engagement. This appointment is significant for investors, as it could influence how effectively the company navigates policy shifts, device regulations, and reimbursement frameworks, which are crucial for a medical equipment business. While the company's 1-year return is positive, its longer-term returns are modest, underscoring the importance of this new role in managing external relationships that affect profitability and market access.

feed.title: Blackstone Private Credit Fund reported total return of -0.4% in February - FT
feed.summary: The Blackstone Private Credit Fund experienced a total return of -0.4% in February, according to an FT report. This information is part of broader news concerning Blackstone Inc., which specializes in managing investment funds and real estate assets. The company's stock, BX, saw a slight decrease of -2.68% at market close on March 20, 2026.

feed.title: Chubb Ltd stock gains spotlight as lead underwriter in major Gulf maritime insurance facility
feed.summary: Chubb Ltd has been selected as the lead underwriter for a U.S. government-backed $20 billion maritime insurance facility for the Strait of Hormuz, a critical role amidst escalating geopolitical tensions. The company reported strong financial performance in 2025, with core operating income of nearly $10 billion, and its stock is endorsed by Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway, which holds an $11 billion stake. Chubb's status as a dividend aristocrat and its low volatility make it an attractive option for DACH investors seeking stable returns in uncertain markets, despite ongoing risks like catastrophe exposure and competition.

feed.title: Halliburton First Quarter 2026 Earnings Conference Call
feed.summary: Halliburton Company announced it will host a conference call on Tuesday, April 21, 2026, at 8:00 a.m. CT (9:00 a.m. ET) to discuss its first-quarter 2026 financial results. A press release detailing the earnings will be issued prior to the call and made available on the company's website. The call will also be accessible via live webcast, with a recorded version available for seven days afterward.

feed.title: Booking Holdings Inc stock faces valuation scrutiny amid resilient travel demand and Q1 2026 anticipation
feed.summary: Booking Holdings Inc.'s stock experienced a dip despite strong Q4 2025 results and robust travel demand, leading to ongoing valuation debates. While some models suggest the stock is undervalued, market performance shows cooled momentum year-to-date compared to broader indices. Investors are closely watching the upcoming Q1 2026 earnings for further sentiment indication, with DACH investors particularly interested in its exposure to the resilient European tourism market.

feed.title: Permian Basin Royalty Trust (NYSE:PBT) to Issue $0.01 Monthly Dividend
feed.summary: Permian Basin Royalty Trust (NYSE: PBT) has declared a monthly dividend of $0.0107 per share, payable on April 14th to shareholders of record as of March 31st. This dividend provides an annualized yield of about 0.6%. The trust, established in 1980, operates as a passive investment vehicle, holding overriding royalty interests in West Texas oil and gas properties without engaging in exploration or production.

feed.title: Lennox International, Inc. (NYSE:LII) Plans Quarterly Dividend of $1.30
feed.summary: Lennox International, Inc. (NYSE:LII) announced a quarterly dividend of $1.30 per share, payable on April 15th to shareholders of record on March 31st. The company has a strong dividend history, having raised it for 16 consecutive years with a low payout ratio of 20.6%. Despite recent mixed financial results, with a Q4 EPS and revenue miss, analysts maintain a "Hold" rating with an average price target of $579.64.

feed.title: EyePoint Sues Ocular Therapeutix Over Alleged Defamation
feed.summary: EyePoint Pharmaceuticals has filed a lawsuit against Ocular Therapeutix, alleging defamation, commercial disparagement, and tortious interference due to false statements made by Ocular Therapeutix about EyePoint's lead retinal drug candidate, DURAVYU. EyePoint is seeking injunctive relief, a public retraction, and monetary damages. Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, rates EYPT as Neutral due to strong program momentum offset by significant financial constraints, including widening losses and heavy cash burn.

feed.title: Nexstar Announces Major Notes Offering for TEGNA Acquisition
feed.summary: Nexstar Media Group's subsidiary, Nexstar Media Inc., is launching a private offering of $3.39 billion in senior secured notes and $1.725 billion in senior unsecured notes to refinance debt related to its acquisition of TEGNA. The proceeds will be used to repay bridge and existing debt, fund the purchase of TEGNA's 2029 notes, and redeem Nexstar Media's 2027 notes, thereby restructuring the combined company's capital. Nexstar also released preliminary pro forma financials for investors, emphasizing their illustrative nature.

feed.title: CVS (CVS) director John Gallina granted 582 shares as semi-annual retainer
feed.summary: CVS Health Corp director John E. Gallina received 582 shares of CVS common stock on March 19, 2026, as a semi-annual retainer under the 2017 Incentive Compensation Plan. The grant was valued at $71.86 per share, and following this transaction, Gallina directly owns 582 CVS shares. This filing indicates a compensation-related equity award rather than an open-market purchase.

feed.title: Howmet Aerospace Inc stock dips amid strong earnings and raised guidance as analysts lift targets
feed.summary: Howmet Aerospace Inc's stock dipped on the NYSE despite exceeding Q4 earnings estimates and raising its full-year 2026 guidance, driven by strong demand for aerospace components. Analysts responded by increasing price targets, viewing the company as a key player in the aviation recovery and capacity expansion. The article also highlights the company's strong financial health and strategic positioning in the engine products sector.

feed.title: NextNRG Disclosed Failure to Satisfy a Continued Listing Rule or Standard
feed.summary: NextNRG (NXXT) has received a notice from Nasdaq for failing to meet the $1.00 minimum bid price requirement. The company has 180 days, until September 14, 2026, to regain compliance, with a possibility of an extension. Trading of NXXT shares continues on the Nasdaq Capital Market.

feed.title: Labcorp Secures $750 Million Unsecured Term Loan Maturing 2028 With Wells Fargo
feed.summary: Labcorp has secured a new $750 million senior unsecured term loan credit agreement, fully funded on March 20, 2026, and maturing on March 20, 2028. The loan, which bears interest at a SOFR-based rate plus 0.700% or a base rate plus 0%, is expected to enhance the company's liquidity and financial flexibility. Wells Fargo Bank is the administrative agent for the facility.

feed.title: Fidelity National Info Announces Chief Product Technology Officer Resignation
feed.summary: Fidelity National Information Services (FIS) announced that its Chief Product Technology Officer, Firdaus Bhathena, will resign effective March 20, 2026. This leadership change comes as FIS, a global fintech provider, faces a "Neutral" rating from TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, influenced by strong cash generation and improved balance sheet but tempered by weak technical signals and a high P/E ratio. The most recent analyst rating on FIS stock is a Buy with a $69.00 price target.

feed.title: Labcorp (NYSE: LH) secures $750M unsecured term loan due 2028
feed.summary: Labcorp Holdings Inc. (NYSE: LH) has secured a new $750 million senior unsecured term loan facility, maturing on March 20, 2028. The loan, fully funded at closing, features a floating interest rate tied to SOFR or a base rate, plus a margin. The agreement includes customary covenants limiting additional debt and liens, and requires Labcorp to maintain a consolidated leverage ratio not exceeding 4.0 to 1.0, with a temporary increase to 4.5 to 1.0 allowed for material acquisitions under a "Leverage Holiday" provision.

feed.title: Oceaneering (OII) revises change-of-control terms and adopts new ELT severance plan
feed.summary: Oceaneering International, Inc. (OII) has updated its executive protection arrangements, including amending the change of control agreement for CEO Roderick A. Larson and the company-wide Change of Control Plan. The updates introduce conditions such as claim releases, restrictive covenants, and prorated incentives. Additionally, the Compensation Committee adopted a new Executive Leadership Team Severance Plan, providing benefits to eligible executives whose employment is terminated without cause or for good reason, subject to similar conditions.

feed.title: Inside NMP Acquisition Corp. (Nasdaq: NMP) 2025 SPAC structure, trust and timeline
feed.summary: This article provides an in-depth overview of NMP Acquisition Corp. (NMP), a blank check company formed in 2024, detailing its 2025 SPAC structure, IPO, trust account management, and timeline for completing a business combination. It outlines the company's acquisition strategy, target selection criteria, and mechanisms for shareholder redemption, emphasizing potential conflicts of interest among management and the 18-month deadline for a successful merger or liquidation. The report also addresses financial position, liquidity, related-party transactions, and accounting standards, including the company's status as an emerging growth company.

feed.title: Keurig Dr Pepper plant outside Windsor again under contract
feed.summary: The former Keurig Dr Pepper roasting plant near Windsor, Virginia, is under contract for sale again, with an expected closing by the end of March. This marks a new attempt to sell the property after a previous deal with Schenley Investments collapsed six months prior. The plant, which ceased production in April 2025, is a significant industrial property in the area and its sale is part of broader economic development efforts in Isle of Wight County.

feed.title: Tax-driven stock sale by Pangaea (PANL) CFO after RSU vesting
feed.summary: Pangaea Logistics Solutions Ltd. CFO Gianni DelSignore sold 23,779 common shares at a weighted average price of $6.99 per share on March 18 and 19, 2026. The sale was conducted to cover tax withholding obligations associated with the vesting of restricted stock units. DelSignore retains a significant stake in the company, holding 378,668 common shares after this transaction.

feed.title: Citigroup Lowers Price Target for Delta Air Lines (DAL) to $77
feed.summary: Citigroup analyst John Godyn has lowered the price target for Delta Air Lines (DAL) from $87.00 to $77.00, while maintaining a "Buy" rating. This adjustment aligns with a recent trend of other analysts also reducing their price targets for DAL in March 2026. Despite these reductions, the recurring "Buy" ratings suggest continued confidence in Delta's core performance.

feed.title: Orion Energy Systems & Unit Enters Settlement Agreement With Final Frontier And Kathleen M. Connors - SEC Filing
feed.summary: Orion Energy Systems and a unit have entered into a settlement agreement with Final Frontier and Kathleen M. Connors, as detailed in an SEC filing. The article, sourced from Reuters, briefly notes the development and is relevant to mergers and acquisitions activity and US stocks.

feed.title: International Business Machines (NYSE:IBM) Shares Down 1.7% Following Analyst Downgrade
feed.summary: International Business Machines (IBM) shares dropped 1.7% after BMO Capital Markets downgraded the stock to "market perform" and cut its price target from $350 to $290. Despite a "Moderate Buy" consensus among analysts, several firms have lowered price targets or ratings due to various factors. However, IBM's fundamentals remain strong, with recent earnings beating estimates, a 2.7% dividend yield, and insider buying activity.

feed.title: OFG Bancorp (OFG) awards 2,145 restricted units to risk chief
feed.summary: OFG Bancorp's Chief Risk Officer, Cesar A. Ortiz, was granted 2,145 restricted units tied to common stock as compensation under the company's performance incentive plan. This award increases his total holdings to 4,182 restricted units. The units will vest in three equal annual installments over the next three years, with the underlying common shares to be received at the end of the restricted period.

feed.title: Verizon Communications (NYSE:VZ) Shares Up 1.2% After Analyst Upgrade
feed.summary: Verizon Communications (NYSE:VZ) shares rose 1.2% after Citigroup upgraded its price target from $50.00 to $55.00, maintaining a "buy" rating. The company also increased its quarterly dividend to $0.7075, resulting in an annualized dividend of $2.83 and a yield of approximately 5.7%. Despite insider sales totaling over $10 million in the past three months, institutional investors have increased their holdings, with the company reporting strong Q4 earnings, surpassing analyst estimates.

feed.title: After 23 years on Pentair's board, David Jones hands chair to FedEx veteran
feed.summary: David A. Jones, Chair of Pentair's Board of Directors, will retire on May 5, 2026, after 23 years of service to the company, including eight as Chairman. T. Michael Glenn, a retired Executive Vice President from FedEx Corporation, will succeed Jones as Chair. Glenn has been an independent director on Pentair's Board since 2007 and has played a significant role in the company's strategic and marketing initiatives.

feed.title: Arista Networks (NYSE:ANET) Shares Down 3.8% Following Insider Selling
feed.summary: Arista Networks (NYSE:ANET) shares dropped 3.8% after disclosures of significant insider selling by Kenneth Duda and Director Yvonne Wassenaar. Despite the insider sales, the company maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, with a target price of $176.47, and recently surpassed its Q4 earnings and revenue estimates. The stock showed increased trading volume on the day of the drop, indicating heightened investor activity in response to the news.

feed.title: Palisade Bio (NASDAQ:PALI) Issues Quarterly Earnings Results, Beats Estimates By $1.10 EPS
feed.summary: Palisade Bio (NASDAQ:PALI) reported quarterly earnings of $1.08 EPS, significantly beating analyst estimates of ($0.02) by $1.10, yet its stock fell 5% to $1.89 on the trading day. The clinical-stage biotech, focusing on localized immunotherapies for cancer, has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts with an average target price of $12.33. Institutional investors hold approximately 11.79% of the company's stock.

feed.title: San Diego Gas & Electric closes $1.1 billion bond offering
feed.summary: San Diego Gas & Electric, a subsidiary of Sempra, has successfully closed a $1.1 billion public offering of first mortgage bonds. The offering included two series of bonds maturing in 2036 and 2056 with interest rates of 5.200% and 5.950% respectively. This strategic financial move is expected to support the company's capital needs, with Sempra demonstrating financial stability through consistent dividend increases and payments.

feed.title: KOLLEEN DIBBLE, 34
feed.summary: This article is a brief profile of Kolleen Dibble, aged 34, who holds the position of Regional Vice President for the facsimile division at Pitney Bowes Inc. The content is very minimal, essentially serving as a directory entry or a quick highlight.

feed.title: J.P. Morgan upgrades Nomura Holdings (NRSCF) to a Buy
feed.summary: J.P. Morgan has upgraded Nomura Holdings (NRSCF) to a Buy rating. This upgrade comes with a new price target from the analyst. Another firm, TipRanks – OpenAI’s OpenAI Financial – Capital Markets, also issued a Buy rating for Nomura Holdings, while TipRanks – xAI reiterated a Hold rating on the same day.

feed.title: Pfizer warns shareholders against below-market mini-tender offer
feed.summary: Pfizer Inc. has issued a warning to its shareholders regarding an unsolicited mini-tender offer from Tutanota LLC to purchase up to 1 million shares at $32.00 per share. Pfizer advises shareholders against tendering their shares, citing the below-market pricing condition and Tutanota's lack of financing, and notes that the current market price for Pfizer shares is significantly lower than the offer's conditional threshold. The company also highlighted recent positive Phase 2 clinical trial results for its breast cancer drug candidate, atirmociclib, and its atopic dermatitis drug, tilrekimig, which analysts believe provides potential upside.

feed.title: Honeywell's $7.6B Debt Redemption & Refinancing Strategy | 2026 - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Honeywell International Inc. announced it will redeem approximately $7.6 billion in debt, denominated in both U.S. dollars and euros, following a tender offer. This action comes after the company recently issued $16 billion in new senior notes and is occurring as Honeywell prepares to spin off its aerospace business. Some market observers view this as a refinancing or rolling over of existing obligations rather than a pure debt reduction, with concerns about borrowing costs and the timing of using newly raised capital.

feed.title: Evercore and Goldman Sachs Stay Bullish on Arista Networks (ANET)
feed.summary: Evercore ISI and Goldman Sachs have maintained bullish stances on Arista Networks (ANET), a cloud networking company, due to its strong position in the growing AI infrastructure market. Evercore reiterated an Outperform rating and $200 price target, citing ANET's benefit from a major 6-gigawatt agreement between AMD and Meta Platforms. Goldman Sachs raised its price target from $165 to $188, maintaining a Buy rating, expecting strong double-digit growth in revenue and EPS driven by data demand, cloud migration, and network infrastructure investments.

feed.title: NextEra Energy (NYSE: NEE) issues $600M 6.50% junior debentures
feed.summary: NextEra Energy Capital Holdings, a wholly owned subsidiary of NextEra Energy (NYSE: NEE), has issued $600 million of Series Z Junior Subordinated Debentures due April 15, 2086, with a fixed interest rate of 6.50% per year. These debentures are guaranteed on a subordinated basis by NextEra Energy and can be redeemed by NEECH starting in April 2031. The issuance was filed under a Form 8-K to report this material event and provide related legal opinions.

feed.title: US appeals court tosses FTC order against Intuit over TurboTax advertising
feed.summary: A U.S. appeals court has overturned a Federal Trade Commission (FTC) order that prohibited Intuit from deceptively advertising its TurboTax products as "free" for simple tax returns. The 5th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals ruled that it violated the constitutional separation of powers for an administrative law judge to decide a deceptive advertising claim. This decision effectively dismisses the FTC's previous injunction against Intuit's advertising practices.

feed.title: Family custodian reports multiple MHK share sales (NYSE: MHK)
feed.summary: A family custodian, LORBERBAUM FAMILY PAST S HELEN CUST, has filed a Form 144 with the SEC, indicating proposed sales of common stock for Mohawk Industries Inc. (NYSE: MHK). The filing details multiple transactions from late February to March 2026, including specific sales on March 10, 2026, and March 19, 2026, with their corresponding proceeds. The article emphasizes that this is a neutral event with no positive or negative impact disclosed.

feed.title: OFG Bancorp (NYSE: OFG) CEO receives 14,390 restricted stock units award
feed.summary: Jose Rafael Fernandez, CEO and Chairman of the Board of OFG Bancorp, received an award of 14,390 Restricted Units, with each unit tied to an equal number of common stock shares. This grant was made under the company's Amended and Restated 2007 Omnibus Performance Incentive Plan. The Restricted Period for these units will lapse in three equal annual installments over three years, starting from the February 26, 2026 award date.

feed.title: Alphabet Stock 2026 Outlook: AI Investment Impact and Price Target - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Alphabet's stock saw a 65% increase in 2025 but has declined by 3% in early 2026. The company is investing significantly (between $175 billion and $185 billion) in AI, which is expected to impact profitability due to higher costs. Despite this, with a P/E multiple of 28 and projected earnings growth, analysts believe the stock could reach $350 by year-end.

feed.title: Mohawk Industries Insider Sold Shares Worth $581,950, According to a Recent SEC Filing
feed.summary: An insider at Mohawk Industries (MHK) recently sold shares worth $581,950, as reported in a new SEC filing. This follows another insider share sale of $416,200 on March 18. Mohawk Industries is a global flooring manufacturer, and the company recently reported Q4 2025 earnings that beat estimates.

feed.title: Bristol Myers Squibb Transforms the Classical Hodgkin Lymphoma Treatment Paradigm with Expanded U.S. and EMA Approvals for Opdivo® (nivolumab)
feed.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb has received expanded U.S. and EU approvals for its drug Opdivo (nivolumab) in treating classical Hodgkin Lymphoma (cHL). In the U.S., Opdivo in combination with AVD is approved for previously untreated Stage III or IV cHL in patients 12 and older, based on the Phase 3 SWOG 1826 study. The EU approval, based on the Phase 2 CheckMate-744 study, allows Opdivo in combination with brentuximab vedotin for relapsed or refractory cHL in certain pediatric and adult patients after one prior line of therapy. These approvals mark a significant advancement, offering new immunotherapy combinations for cHL patients.
